items.headline: State grant awarded to Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts
items.summary: The City Council approved receipt of a California Natural Resources Agency grant for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. The funding will support arts and cultural programming at this South Bay institution.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council received Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, which includes revised scoring criteria and plan edits. This plan guides investments in biking and pedestrian infrastructure across Cupertino.

items.headline: City Council Changes Vacancy Filling Process
items.summary: The council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled, reducing procedures related to filling empty seats. This could affect how quickly the city fills council positions when members leave office.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrade project approved downtown
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project is underway at 1290 Ridder Park Dr. This significant commercial infrastructure investment suggests upgrades to an older office or mixed-use building in the Ridder Park area.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition suing city over Mary Avenue safety issues
items.summary: The city is defending against litigation from the Garden Gate Coalition regarding safety concerns on Mary Avenue. This ongoing case involves residents' complaints about traffic or pedestrian safety on that street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial or industrial project is approved at 1300 W San Carlos St. This represents new business activity in San José's industrial corridor along West San Carlos.

items.headline: Gas utility rates and reserves adjusted for 2027
items.summary: The Council approved changes to Palo Alto's residential and commercial gas service rates for fiscal year 2027, along with adjustments to utility reserves. This will affect what residents and businesses pay for natural gas service.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a Business License Amnesty Program and new economic development incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups. The city is also launching outreach to shopping centers and a 'Shop Local' campaign.
